TextENGNew: In this dark corner of the park draped in only the curtain of light coming from the streetlights...Tomoe suddenly adjusted her posture to look face to face with Reina Ryuugu.
The girl stood up from the bench, with an expression so distraught it was plain to see even in the dark, and looked at Tomoe with her jaw clenched tight.
"A detective...? From Ibaraki prefecture...?!"
"Yes, that's right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2005. Maybe I forgot to mention that when we met at the phone booth."
"...What are you talking about?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1046. Rena has never met you before."
"Oh really now...?"
Looking at Reina's face, Tomoe snorted and shrugged her shoulders.
To hear her so completely deny it after leaving such a strong impression wasn't just brazen, it was aggravating.
What's more, the fact that she could remain so calm while lying was far beyond her years, and not like an innocent child at all.
(Is she pretending it never happened?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2008. ...Fine, even if that's the case, I'll peel back her layers.)
Tomoe set aside the discomfort in her heart and carefully focused on Reina's mannerisms as she stood there quietly.
She too slow to react last year, but now that she knew her opponent was capable of behaving aggressively, she had no intention of repeating the same mistake.
For that reason, she was willing to set aside her dignity as a police officer and pin her down if things got a little rough.

"Well, let's just put that aside.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2010. ...That said, I'm not a total stranger.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2011. The juvenile division officer who interrogated you was actually a friend of mine... and I've heard a lot of stories."
Reina grabbed the ribbon on her chest and squeezed it hard enough to let out an audible squeak, while cautiously gazing at Tomoe.
Seeing that, Tomoe casually relaxed the left side of her jacket, ready to immediately pull the extendable baton from her inner pocket depending on Reina's reaction.
(You won't take me by surprise this time...)

Plus, even if she tried to flee, Tomoe could apprehend her on the grounds of trying to guide a girl loitering around outside in the middle of the night... and justify her actions that way. Tomoe remained on full alert, and wouldn't let her advantageous position go to waste.
"...Do you want something?"
"Let me turn it around and ask you the same thing.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2014. You live all the way in Hinamizawa, so what business do you have here in Kakiuchi...?"
Reina turned back with a sharp glare, while suddenly catching her breath and solidifying herself.
...That stern reaction was far different from how schoolchildren are raised to react. It was far more intimidating than words, enough to make you forget you were dealing with another girl.
"...Is something wrong?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2016. How about this, if I answer your question first, will you answer mine...?"
"Rena... just came here to see a friend.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1051. And stopped at this park on the way back..."
"I see.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2018. A friend from your days in Ibaraki... I wonder if you're referring to Nagisa Ozaki?"
Now completely understanding everything, huge globs of sweat began pouring out of Reina's entire body all at once.
This detective named Minai wasn't simply asking questions. She was verifying the information she already knew...!
(Was she following me...?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1054. For how long?!)
She went from Hinamizawa to Okinomiya, then took a bus to Kakiuchi. She was thinking about Nagisa the whole time, so she never noticed she was being followed.
What's more, based on her responses it was clear she'd been following her well before she came to the park.
But... why on earth would someone follow her?
Even though she only left Hinamizawa to meet up with Nagisa, why would someone be keeping watch of her like this?
Against that completely illogical absurdity, confusion and hesitation ran rampant. ...And from that thought, animosity and distrust toward this detective rose up in Reina's chest.
Just what on earth was this detective questioning her about...?
"...Do you have business with Ozaki-san?"
"Not really... Nothing that would concern a detective.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1056. ...It's getting really late, so can I go home how?"
"...Please wait."
After trying to leave, Tomoe quickly grabbed Reina by the arm to stop her. Reina instinctively tried to shake it off, but moaned while turning to her with a look of agony on her face.
Her wrist joint was lightly bent inwards. If she showed any signs of resistance, she could be easily suppressed in a single blow...!
(This lady... is serious...!)
A horrifying fright rose up inside of her, but Reina suppressed the trembling in her voice and finally said "...please let go."
...However, Tomoe maintained her stern expression and continued speaking.

"...When minors are loitering in the middle of the night, police officers have the right to take them into protective custody at their discretion.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2022. Considering the time and the circumstances of this town, it wouldn't be unreasonable."
"And let me just tell you, if you try to fight back here you could be adding obstruction of government duties to the mix.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2024. ...For your sake, I suggest you quietly comply."
The light in Reina's eyes was shaking violently in fear. When Tomoe was able to see that in the dim light... she suddenly frowned in discomfort.
...When she made that sort of proclamation with young people in the past, there were two main types of reactions.
The first is to become hostile, to show intense emotions like a beast baring its fangs.
This is the general attitude one shows when they realize they're in an inferior position, and the fear and anxiety visible in the back of their eyes is clear proof of that.
And the second is to become entirely expressionless, as if completely frozen... but also completely observant of the movements around you, waiting for a chance to act without hesitation.

The latter of which is far more cunning and harder to deal with. They lose perception of evil in their own situation and they even abandon any feelings of guilt, so they're at an elevated risk of violently running wild.
Even now, Tomoe still suspected that Reina Ryuugu had some involvement in Nagisa Ozaki and Kouhei Sawamura's incidents.

Of course, she didn't think Reina was the culprit. ...But she felt that there was somebody working behind her... whether it was the Sonozaki family like Ooishi says, or some sort of medical personnel, or something else.
It's possible that those people were acting behind the scenes, and when Reina entered this park, they were planning to make contact with her... She couldn't help but speculate.
Thus, in her point of view, Reina Ryuugu should have something to show in this provocation. And once Tomoe saw those signs, she intended to take her into custody no questions asked.
...But even in this darkness, the dim light from the streetlights revealed fear and anxiety on Reina's face.
Moreover, she couldn't detect any of the violent murderous intent she witnessed before, which left Tomoe baffled.
(Is this girl... really that Reina Ryuugu?)
Tomoe squinted her eyes while dubiously looking at Reina.
She expected total resistance, but this was completely different from the image of her from Tomoe's memories.
She gently stroked her neck with her still bandaged hand.
Tomoe definitely was assaulted by her and strangled at the phone booth in Hinamizawa, half a year ago.
"Ahahahahahahahaha.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1062. ...Die, you idiot.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1063. Ahahahahahahaha, ahahahahahahahahahahahah!!!"
Though she was surprised... at the time, she was truly prepared for death.
And in her rapidly fading consciousness, the girl's visage was so frightening and cruel, she looked like a man-eating monster with the blood of demons flowing through her, just as Ooishi said.
...And after that, on the night when she went out to eat with her little sister Madoka, she saw Reina at the scene when their car exploded.
Tomoe still had some lingering doubts about how she'd be able to cause a car to explode, but since she was there it was hard to say she was irrelevant.
And yet, the Reina Ryuugu standing right in front of her... was a completely ordinary girl.
(...Which of them is the real Reina Ryuugu?)
Tomoe remained alert to her surroundings while keeping her eyes fixed on Reina. ...She couldn't see any other people nearby or sense any signs of anyone else approaching.
In the worst-case scenario, she was prepared to be attacked by the girl's contact when she first called out to her, but now she was starting to feel a bit let down.
"...Anyway, would you mind coming down to the station?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2031. I'll need to contact your parents, too."

"But why?!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1065. Rena hasn't done anything wrong!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1066. I already spoke to my Dad about it, and I was planning to head straight home!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1067. You can't just arbitrarily decide I'm up to no good!!"

"...Arbitrarily decide?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2033. But you can't say there's no basis for that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2034. It doesn't matter if you're a child or an adult when you have a "criminal record.""

"For an ordinary child in this situation, I'd simply tell them to be careful.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2036. ...But any police officer who knows your past would almost certainly take it into consideration.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2037. In which case, maybe we shouldn't just contact your parents, but also your current school?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2038. ...Be mindful of your carelessness."
Though she realized it was a harsh thing to say, Tomoe said it anyway in an attempt to shake Reina up.

If she was actually up to no good, this was the point where she'd turn defiant or begin acting violently. That way, her passion would give a glimpse of her real intentions regardless of how she tried to portray herself.
But... what if that wasn't the case? Perhaps she was doing the most heinous thing a former youth division officer could ever do?
In that way, her suspicion turned into self-questioning, and then self-loathing... but just then.

"Why do police officers keep talking bad about Rena like that?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1072. Despite everything she did, Rena has... I have a lot of regrets... and I'm trying to start over, to be reborn."
"...The happiness I destroyed before can never be regained, but in that case I've been trying my best to find new happiness, and to cherish it this time."
"...Yet, why can't you police officers understand that?"
"...Well."
"Or is that still not enough?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1076. Can't you see I'm feeling remorse and atoning for my sins?"
"Then what kind of punishment must I endure before you'll understand?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1078. ...Or do I simply no longer have a right to be happy?"
Reina's accusations had Tomoe at a loss for words.
There was no anger or aggression in her voice... In fact, all that was there was the sound of one being torn apart by grief.
Regret for her sins. Sorrow for her past. And a sense of loss.
"...I already understand.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1080. The friends I hurt and the people I caused trouble for are suffering and sadder than I ever could be."
"It's a cowardly, shameless, despicable act to make a mess of things and then run away like you had nothing to do with it."

"...Even if I apologize now, it's already too late.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1083. Nobody will forgive me.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1084. And if something happens, I'll be the first they suspect.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1085. No matter what excuses I make, they won't believe me that easily..."
"Ryuugu-san..."

"But... but if that's the case... what am I supposed to do?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1087. Fall to pieces like the glass I shattered?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1088. If I suffered the same wounds as the friends I hurt, would that be enough to satisfy everyone?"
"...In that case, why didn't you do it?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1090. Even though... I should've received the same punishment for the crimes I committed...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1091. Why...?! Why!?!"
Reina's voice was gradually getting lower, heavier, and more murky... Her eyes had previously been staring intently at Tomoe, but were now gazing downward.
And then, when the sniffling soreness in her throat reached her ears... she looked up, with tears overflowing from her dark eyes.
As soon as Tomoe released her hand, Reina collapsed on the spot, sobbing with her hands over her face.
Looking down at her like that... Tomoe felt intense regret and reproach after realizing she had made such a tremendous misunderstanding and mistake all this time.
Since the girl had attacked her, she'd thought her heartless.
She thought of her as the girl who tried to bomb her car, so she couldn't view her appropriately as a girl of her age.
(But... that's not right. That wasn't the case...)
Reina Ryuugu. ...She too was still suffering from her past.
Always thinking of the sins she committed, the irreversible mistakes she made... and constantly feeling a sense of guilt over the fact that she never received the punishment she deserved.
Based on what Ooishi said, she had a bright, cheerful demeanor when she was spending time with her classmates... but on the other hand, she also had very unusual hobbies.
At first glance, those two notions seemed completely contradictory... but tracing them back, they both originated from the same general ideas.
Repenting for her past.
Focusing on the present.
And anxiety about the future...
"...Ryuugu-san."
If you're unable to bear the pain and suffering of your wounds, is it cowardly to look away? Is it unfair?
...Tomoe had no right to say one way or the other.
And if that's the case, then...
"I still hate her and resent what she did... but in the end, I still want to believe in her."

"And this time, I want to do everything I can.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1034. Talk to each other, argue with each other... and I want to finally forgive her.@k@vS14/35/VT4Bb_nagisa1035. That's how I truly feel."
"...I'm sorry."

"I got carried away and said some things I shouldn't have.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2049. You've already endured enough punishment... There was no need for me to keep tearing you down like that.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2050. ...I'm really sorry."
After saying that, Tomoe released all her tension, then walked over to Reina and knelt down to gently hand her a handkerchief.
She just stared at it for a while... but eventually, she quietly grabbed it and wiped away her nonstop overflowing tears.
"...You came to see Nagisa Ozaki-san, right?"
Reina's head was turned down and she was choking back sobs as she lightly nodded to Tomoe.
...Seeing her up close, her body looked very innocent and delicate.
There was no trace of the criminal from the assault incident who sent several students to the hospital. She was a completely ordinary girl.
(Her reactions... don't seem like acting.)
Of course... there was a non-zero chance that this was all camouflage, and that her real intentions were far darker.
Nevertheless, Tomoe couldn't imagine that a girl exuding such heartfelt sentiment could be so shrewd.
...No, maybe she didn't want to think so. Because if she doubted even that, she wouldn't be able to believe in anything in the future...
"...Nagisa-san was something of an acquaintance of mine.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2054. She wanted to meet you, very much."
"She came to me asking for advice about it.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2056. She was so happy when she received that call from you."
"Nagisa-chan was..."
Reina pulled the soaked handkerchief away from her eyes and stared at Tomoe, who was gazing at her with a far gentler expression than before.
She able to set aside her caution because Tomoe was sincere enough to kneel down on the soil to match eye level with her, showing no concern over getting dirty in the process.
And also because...
(Nagisa-chan knows this lady...?)
The Nagisa that Reina knew was very cheerful but also very shy. No matter the age of the person she was speaking to, she wasn't one to casually reveal secrets like that.
So, if Nagisa told her about the phone call from Reina, she must've really trusted her.

"So, well... I think I was more angry with you than she was.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2058. I was thinking "She must be a terrible girl to cut all ties with and vanish from someone as kind and caring about her friends as Nagisa-san.""

"...I got that idea in my head without trying to get to know you.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2060. That's why I was speaking to you with a negative outlook even from the beginning.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2061. ...I'm sorry."
"Detective..."
Upon seeing Tomoe plainly and sincerely bow her head in front of her, Reina stopped crying and began to calm herself.
...She felt like she could understand why Nagisa thought so highly of this detective.
"......I wanted to apologize..."
Then, opening her heart.
Reina spat out the words that she'd been holding in all this time.
"Really, I... I wanted to apologize, so so much...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1101. I did something terrible... and caused Nagisa-chan so much pain..."

"I hit the boy she loved over and over with a bat, and I seriously wounded him...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1103. I went on a rampage through the school building... Even though she was crying and telling me to stop, I shattered the windows and smashed the walls, making a total mess of the place...!"
Even now... she sometimes saw it in her dreams.
Shattered window glass covered the linoleum floor of the hallway.
Her whole body was covered in scratches. She walked around with a vacant look in her eyes, and her uniform dyed in blood.
She couldn't remember where she found it, but she was holding a metal bat.
Reina held it in one hand, dragging it along the ground... letting out a dry rattling sound as she went.
The classmates around her all watched with terrified looks in their eyes, unable to even approach her.

The adults didn't know what to do. Not even the physically strong PE teacher or the annoyingly cautious older teacher... They just tried to maintain their dignity by protecting the students from her violence.
Reina gazed at that pathetic sight... and sneered.
Then let out a high-pitched laugh to scoff at them.
A cruel, distorted look floated to the edge of her mouth, like she was toying with them.
"Ahahahahahahaha...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1105. Aahahahahahahahaha...!!!"
...It was horrifying.

As she continued her violent assault... she kept on cackling without showing any hesitation or kindness, like some kind of monster... Her head felt pain like it was being split open, so bad that intense vomit came rising up from the back of her throat.
And what's more... that face truly was hers.
Even though her hair was a mess and she was caked in blood and mud, those eyes, that nose, and that mouth were just like looking in a mirror.
...No, that's not it.
That... was undoubtedly her former self.
It left an ugly mark like a nail hammered into her memory... Reina herself was the thing she most hated in the world, and most wanted to forget...
"I... I don't even know why I did that... Honestly, I have no idea!"
"But I completely understand that I did something unreasonable, and I've regretted it a lot since them. And yet there's no way to undo something like that!"
"I kept thinking about how much I wanted to apologize to everyone, but I realized nobody would ever forgive me, so I got more and more scared...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1109. And then... and then!!"
"...Ryuugu-san."
"I really... really wanted to apologize!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1111. I wanted to see all the people I caused trouble for and apologize from the bottom of my heart, as soon as possible!"

"But... but when I thought about my most precious friend telling me I was a terrible person... and that she wanted me to die... I... I...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1113. I was afraid of being scorned, or being hated... So... so...!!"
"I understand.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2065. ...No, I think I want to understand that feeling.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2066. Being hated by your dear friends... is truly painful."
Tomoe gently laid her hand on Reina's dirty, tear-stained hands. An endlessly spilling stream of tears dripped from above, conveying the depth of her sorrow.
...How could such an innocent girl have resorted to that level of violence? The confusion and doubts still smoldered in her heart like a small fire.
However.
In that case, she had to prove these tears were genuine. ...Thinking that, Tomoe raised her head and called out "Ryuugu-san."
"...Let's go meet her."
"I'll take you there.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2070. ...To Nagisa-san."
...Tossing around in her sleep, Reina suddenly noticed something unusual beside her and opened her eyes.
She could vaguely recognize the backs of two chairs lined up. And she didn't hear the sound of birds... but the hum of an engine and the sound of flowing wind.
"Did you sleep well?"
"Huh?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1117. Ah..."
Reina jumped up, tossing aside the blanket that had been covering her body.
Even though it was pitch black outside when she dozed off, looking out the windshield past the passenger seat she could see the sun shining bright as it rose beyond the ridgeline of the mountains.
"I... fell asleep?"

"Yes, you were sleeping soundly.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2073. ...You must've been really tired.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2074. It'll still be a while longer before we arrive, so feel free to get a little more rest."
"No... I've had plenty.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1120. Thanks."
Reina neatly folded the rumpled blanket and set it aside, then took a seat. ...Really, she was still feeling sleepy, but she just couldn't relax.
"...Have you been driving this whole time?"

"No way, I took a break at some point.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2076. I stopped at a service area and got ten minutes of rest, or so...@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2077. That's when I gave you that blanket. You don't remember that?"
"...Sorry."
Reina apologetically shrugged her shoulders... but at the same time, she seemed uncomfortable.

It's true that she was worried about her family and about Nagisa, and that she hadn't gotten a good night's rest in a while... but she had never, ever slept that deeply in the back seat of a stranger's car.

Not to mention, she was shown this kindness from a police officer... it was like she was riding in the car of an enemy. Even though it was a relief to learn that she knew Nagisa, for a while after waking up it still felt like a dream.
(...Maybe.)
Suddenly, Reina had a thought. This detective named Minai knew about her past... so she wondered if she was prepared to reopen it?
When she came to Hinamizawa, no matter how much fun she was having with her close friends... somewhere in her heart she had the illusion it was all an act.
That was when she decided to forget her past and start calling herself "Rena Ryuugu."
Someone who likes kyute things, who is kind to everyone, and tries her very best at everything... that was the sort of ideal girl Reina always strived to be.
Thanks to that, she was fortunate enough to gain new friends. And not just people who are fun to play with, but "allies" who are willing to help her when she's in trouble.
Just the other day, they were kind enough to help her before the situation would have become rough at home.
(I think right now... I'm happy.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1125. I understand that much...)

But, on the other hand... she hated the source of all her old problems, the existence known as "Reina Ryuugu." ...And for that reason, she decided that all proof of the over a decade she lived under that old name should disappear.
And... she was afraid of her new friends learning about her time in Ibaraki. So, she started subconsciously developing resistance to letting her true feelings show to them.
(But... that's not right.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1127. That's what everyone taught me...)
Keiichi, Rika, and Mion. They were all worried about her holding everything in.
And Satoko suddenly asked if they could have a sleepover just before the incident, probably because she knew something was wrong.
Plus, Keiichi and the others only learned about the situation with Rina Mamiya because Shion told them in the first place...
She placed a hand on her chest and took a light breath.
...That's right, she had to mark the end of her old self.
It's not about forgetting it or throwing it away. She had to face it steadfastly, take it... and digest it into her current self.
(So, I... decided to go see Nagisa-chan.)
No matter what terrible things she may say, even if she scolded her and vented her rage at her... she didn't mind.
No matter how hard or painful it may be... if I don't start there, I won't be able to move forward.
With that determination lingering in the bottom of her heart... Reina raised her head and looked forward.
"...I'm sorry."
"Huh?"
"For making you take a day off school, and bringing you this far out.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2080. Are you sure you shouldn't tell your teacher?"

"Oh, yes.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1132. Mii-chan... I mean, a friend of mine said she'd explain things.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1133. She told my father I'd be staying at her house, and that we'd go to school together."
"I see..."
Gently gazing at Reina through the rear-view mirror, Tomoe's thoughts were somewhere else.
The "Mii-chan" she referred to was probably her best friend Mion Sonozaki... the acting head of the Sonozaki family and leader of the Three Families.
...But thanks to that, Tomoe had another concern. Being able to invent facts about a student's absence meant that she could also easily manufacture proof of someone's "disappearance."
In fact, she heard from Ooishi... a boy went missing a few days after Watanagashi last year. And he suspected it was a plot orchestrated by the Sonozaki family.
In which case, wouldn't the fact that Reina is spending time with a police officer potentially create unnecessary misunderstandings that could endanger her...?
(...Maybe I'm overthinking it.)
Reina used a payphone to contact her friend Mion, and Tomoe was listening in beside her, but she didn't say anything about the police and they didn't seem to be speaking in code words.
The person on the other end of the phone probably understood that Reina was simply catching up with an old friend, and having so much fun it was worth taking a day off school.
(More importantly, I wonder why I couldn't get in touch with Hanada-kun?)
Unfortunately, the information center was busy, so all she could do was leave a message telling him to contact her.
Nevertheless, it's a little tough not hearing anything from the other side. She was at least able to carry a pager with her back when she worked at Kakiuchi station...
"What is it?"
"Where are you taking me?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1136. We're almost at Tokyo..."
Just as Reina said, the sign they drove past showed the distance to Tokyo.
It was early morning on a weekday, so there wasn't much traffic. They'd be able to reach their destination in just a few hours.
"Just like I told you, we're heading to Nagisa-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2086. It's a little difficult to get there by train."
"...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1138.Umm, wouldn't it be a bother to show up on a weekday?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1139. It would probably be inconvenient for Nagisa-chan, and she should be at school..."
"It'll be all right.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2088. She's always waiting for you."
"...Always."
Eventually, after passing through several toll gates, Tomoe's car turned off the expressway and onto a national highway.
Then, they proceeded lightly down a few roads with minimal traffic, and the scenery gradually transformed from an unfamiliar city to a desolate smalltown landscape.
The number of traffic lights was gradually decreasing. The tall buildings were gone, and there were sometimes even wide fields spread around.
...In a sense, the scenery conveyed a peaceful atmosphere... but for some reason Reina couldn't help but feel a lingering unease.
And then...
"...We're here."
Tomoe pulled her car into a small parking lot and came to a stop in a very quiet rural area with few residential buildings nearby.
After looking at the sign by the entrance... Reina realized the worst scenario she imagined while on the way here... was the truth.
She began trembling...
While grabbing the headrest of the front passenger seat and leaning out from the back seat... Reina's whole body was quivering so intensely she couldn't take a single step.
Because the sign right before her eyes read... "Musashigawa Memorial Park."
The name made it clear this was a cemetery.
(...I thought so.)
Seeing her eyes open wide and her face turn pale... Tomoe was convinced.
This girl... knew nothing about the "current" Nagisa. She hadn't heard anything about it.
"...Not very strongly.@k@vS14/32/VT4Bb_hanada1066. It's more like she was genuinely apathetic to the news rather than just pretending it had nothing to do with her."
Despite his words echoing inside Tomoe's head... the situation actually proved the opposite. But now, she had an obligation to fulfill, which was more important than that.
"Come on, Ryuugu-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2093. Let's go."
"It may be painful, but stay firm.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2095. ...I'm certain she's waiting for you."
"...Right here."
Right before her eyes was a fairly new tombstone.
"Here lies the Ozaki family."
And, the black letters engraved in the tablet beside it hadn't yet been faded by weathering, so they were easy to read...
"It can't be truuuue!!!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1149. Aah, aaaaaaah!!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1150. Uwaaaaaaaahh~~~!!!"
Reina had been frozen in place, but then like flipping a switch she rushed to the side of the grave and shouted so loud it sounded like blood was spewing from her throat.
And then, she clung to the tombstone and rubbed her forehead against it while crying.
"Nagisa-chan, Nagisa-chan!!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1152. Why...? Why...?!"
Reina raised her wet, crying face to once again look at the tablet, and was surprised when she suddenly saw the date.
"Nagisa Ozaki, died October 14th, 1982."
That day... was the same day she promised to meet Nagisa.
Finally realizing why she never came to Okinomiya... Reina clawed at her hair and wailed.
"It can't be... it can't be...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1154. No... no...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1155. Aaaaaaah, aaaaaaahh...!!!"
"Ryuugu-san."
"Why...? Why is Nagisa-chan...?!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1157. How did this happen to Nagisa-chan?!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1158. Please tell me, Detective!!"
"Was she sick?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1160. Was it an accident?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1161. Or maybe... no way, it can't be!!"
Clinging to Tomoe's hand on her shoulder for comfort, Reina squeezed her voice to ask that in a forceful tone.
...If she said it was murder, this girl would likely begin frantically hunting the killer, intending to personally kill them.
So...
"...It was an accident.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2101. She was struck by a car on her way home."
Tomoe herself didn't believe that... but she had no choice but to answer that way. And Reina's reaction was unequivocal proof that she wasn't involved in Nagisa's incident.

Reina slumped down on the spot... and leaned toward the gravestone beside her with a dejected look on her face, reaching out to the tablet with no regard for her skirt getting dirty... just to touch it.
Several difficult kanji were spelled out there detailing a posthumous name, but she didn't understand what they meant. ...She didn't want to.
But, as she came to realize that those characters represented the name of her dear friend, the sadness and despair in her chest grew to crushing levels... and even more tears came overflowing.
"Uuu... gh... uwaaaahh...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1164. ...U... uuu...!!"
"...I wanted to apologize...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1166. I wanted to apologize to Nagisa-chan... for so long...!"
...Reina muttered that in a faint voice that seemed like it was about to fade away. The person to whom she wished to convey those words was no longer in this world.

"I wanted to apologize for so, so long...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1168. To apologize for the terrible things I did, for the awful things I said, and for leaving without saying a word...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1169. And... and then for not saying anything to her afterwards, and then... and then...!!"
"I built up the courage... enough courage to call her on the phone!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1171. When that happened, and I was able to speak with her, I was so, so happy!!"
"I wanted her to tell me we could meet up again... and if we did, I didn't care what she said or did to me... even if she killed me!"
"I thought that... and even thought I was prepared for it, I still wanted to see Nagisa-chan again!!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1174. And yet... and yet...!!"
...The day when they were scheduled to meet one another.
Reina kept waiting for a long, long time.
Time passed all the way into the evening.
Even after dark, after the trains stopped running... she kept on waiting.
...Maybe.
Maybe she got the date wrong, so she thought about waiting until the next day.
But in the end, Nagisa never came...
Reina felt crushed by the weight of her sins... and cried.
She left Okinomiya station and kept on walking and walking, a distance that would take hours by bus... crying all the way.
For the next few days, she was bedridden with a fever.
And when the fever receded, she finally regained a little strength.
Reina thought it over. This... must have been her punishment.
Thinking that, she decided... this pain was a cross she'd have to bear for the rest of her life.
"...Uuuu... uuuuuu...!!"
...However.
Her true "punishment" wasn't that.
What she thought of all this time as a harsh "punishment" actually seemed easy compared to this cruelty...
"Ryuugu-san..."
"...Detective.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1177. What should I do...?"
"Can I no longer be forgiven?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1179. What can I do to make amends for my sins?"
"...Well."

"Should I be sentenced to death?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1181. Should this body suffer the same fate it inflicted on others?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1182. Are criminals forever branded with their crimes no matter what they do?"
"I... don't understand anymore...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1184. What should I do? What is the right thing to do? ...I don't understand anymore...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1185. Uu, waaaaaaaahh...!!"
In response to Reina's heartbroken question... Tomoe could only turn her head down and bite her lip.

It's perfectly natural to punish people for their transgressions. ...But, if there's nobody there to offer them forgiveness, the criminal will be forced to suffer the rest of their life with the weight of their sins on their conscience.
Of course, that could be seen as their true punishment. And in Reina Ryuugu's case, the opportunity to obtain forgiveness has already been lost forever.
There's no greater punishment. And if so, will she have to continue her entire life without ever being forgiven...?
(...That can't be.)
Tomoe shook her head. She was a police officer, not an avenger. The difference between them was that there were other important duties she had a responsibility to fulfill beyond catching criminals.
And that was...
"...Ryuugu-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2110. Read this."
After saying that.
Tomoe pulled a single envelope from her jacket pocket and held it out to Reina.
The address had her name on it. And the sender... was Nagisa.
"What is this...?"
"The very last letter Nagisa-san sent me.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2112. I think... you should read it."
"From Nagisa-chan...?"
She pulled a bundle of notepaper out from the envelope and spread it out before Reina. ...The writing on the page was nostalgic, and could easily be recognized as Nagisa's handiwork.
Reina once again raised her head to look up at Tomoe. Then, after receiving a nod of affirmation, she dropped her eyes back to the paper and started reading...
Dear Detective Minai-sama.
Thank you very much for working so hard and doing so much for me the other day.
I've tried calling you several times since then, but it was always bad timing and you were out, so I've decided to send a letter instead.
In fact, I have something to report.
I was able to talk with Reina over the phone the other day.
It's hard to believe, but I wasn't the one who made the phone call.
Reina was the one who called me.
But it was all too sudden for me, and maybe for Reina too, so it felt a little awkward...
After all this time we didn't know what to talk about, so unfortunately we didn't talk much... but we decided that we should meet up again so we can talk more leisurely.
Why did she cause that incident?
Why did she leave without saying a word about it?
And what did we think of each other now, after living far apart...?
Those are the things I'm thinking of asking when we meet up.
Of course, that may very well reopen old wounds for both Reina and me.
And there's still a chance that meeting her ultimately won't be for the best, so I'm still worried I may regret it.
But I still want to start out by talking with Reina again.
Atonement, confessions...? Those no longer matter to me.
We can talk, and argue, and share everything... but I still want to ask Reina to be my friend.
And I want to make sure I can truly become friends with her this time.
Because I still love Reina.
Reina did cause me sadness, but there are definitely a lot of fun memories we had together.
I want to communicate that to her.
And I want to be able to accept her despite the problems and the pain she caused.
Just like she did for me, so long ago...
Do you think she'll laugh when we met up?
Does she still have that particular speaking habit?
I'm really nervous... but at the same time, I'm full of anticipation.
So I'll believe, and I'll meet with Reina.
And I want to do my best so I can give you good news, Detective.
I'll call you later to share the results.
In case you're busy and I have trouble getting in touch with you again, I wrote down my home phone number. I'd be happy to receive a call any time you'd like.
Anyway, that's all.
Next time we meet, I'll bring you the most beautiful flowers from the gardening club, so look forward to it.
Nagisa Ozaki.
"...Nagisa-chan...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1190. Nagi...sa...chan...!!"
Her dripping tears poured down, leaving stains on the letter.
But... these tears were different from the ones from earlier.
They were purely happy.
Truly grateful.
And also... deeply painful, heartrending, and sad that she wouldn't be able to convey those feelings...!
"I'm sorry... I'm sorry, Nagisa-chan...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1192. I... I always, always wanted to tell you... and yet...!!"
"...I'm sure it made it through to her.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2114. Because Nagisa-san... is your dear friend..."
"...U-uuu... uwaaaaaaahh...!!"
...Thank you.
I'll always, always be grateful to have you as a friend.
Please forgive me... and thank you so much... Nagisa-chan...!
"Ugh... gh... uuuu...!!"
Nagisa's tender thoughts dispelled the darkness in Reina's heart... brightly illuminating it.
...It didn't mean she was forgiven.
That didn't mean her sin was gone.
However.
She found out that someone she loved cared just as deeply about her all this time.
And that alone made her tremendously happy, and grateful...
Reina held the letter against her chest, feeling the warmth of the tears...
"...Ryuugu-san, are you all right?"
Reina wiped her puffy red eyes with the handkerchief over and over again, but she answered back with a nod.
In all likelihood... the sorrow of this day would continue to eat at her heart for a while longer.
Nevertheless, Tomoe believed that Reina Ryuugu would be able to move forward after these wounds healed.
"Atoning for you sin... doesn't mean forgetting it."
"I think forgetting it is the worst thing you can do."
"Because if you forget, you lose the ability to reflect on how your mistakes caused you to behave that way."
"So, you have to remember your sins, to always think strongly about doing good things to make up for your past mistakes."
...Someone once said the more sorrows you experience, the kinder you can be to others. Probably because only then can you truly sympathize with the feelings of sadness in another.

So, no matter how hard it may be... hold on to those memories. Don't forget. And by reflecting on them, you can cherish your precious happiness in the present, and always make the effort to protect it.
"And, in order to keep on remembering... you have to go on living.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2121. Keep on living happily, always. Happy enough to fill her share, too."
"...@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1198.I don't deserve that.@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1199. In fact, someone like me..."
"You will be.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2123. You have to be.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2124. Because otherwise, Nagisa-san was only born into this world to bring you unhappiness."
"Do you wish to look back on the time you spent living with your precious friend and think it was all pointless?"
"Ryuugu-san.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2127. If your conscience is still hounding you... then you should do your best for your other friends, for the sake of that happiness."
"And be able to say what you need to say, so you'll never have any more regrets like you had today... Have the courage to believe you're not alone."
"That way... I can atone for it?"

"Regardless of what the dead may think, your friends will forgive you.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2130. They can appreciate that you did the best you could for yourself, and accept your kindness.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2131. And I, at least, feel that I can forgive you."
Tomoe gently held her hand out as she said that. Reina just stared at it for a while, unable to comprehend what it meant... but after a while, she placed her hand on top and grabbed it back.
"...Okay?"
She still lost and confused... with sadness and guilt continuing to nag at her.
Nevertheless, feeling swept up by Tomoe's words... a gentle smile finally reappeared on Reina's face.
And at the same time, the heavy weight bearing down on her shoulders until now finally disappeared, and a radiant sensation spread all the way to her heart.
Reina and Nagisa. These two close friends could never meet again. ...Thinking about that made Tomoe feel trapped by regret and self-loathing.
But, now... at least for the moment, she wanted to pray for Reina Ryuugu's happiness.
Tomoe believed that until the very end, that's what Nagisa would've wanted for her best friend.
"...Let's head back.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2135. Can you stand?"
"Yes... Ah."
Reina tried to get up, but it was too much for her legs to bear, so she immediately wavered and staggered.
Tomoe tried to support her, but thanks to the momentum they both stumbled and fell to the ground.
"Owww..."
"I-I'm sorry..."
"Oh, it's okay. Don't worry about it."
Reina suddenly looked down and noticed her knees. They must've scraped against the gravel or something when she fell, because she was lightly bleeding.
"Looks like you got hurt.@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2139. Hold on a sec, I'll get you a bandage..."
That moment.
A pill packet spilled out from Tomoe's front pocket. Reina suddenly tilted her head when she saw it... but then she gasped and froze.
"...What is it?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2141. Ryuugu-san, what's wrong?"
"No way... that medicine...?!"
Reina leaned forward and picked up the medicine pack that had fallen to the ground. Then, after flipping it over to confirm the color of the capsules... she opened her eyes even wider in shock.
"I knew it... this is "PC"!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1209. Detective, why do you have this...?!"
"You know this medicine?"
"I... was taking this medicine until a little while ago!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1211. And...?!"
"What is it, Ryuugu-san?"
"This... because of this... I would suddenly lose myself...!@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1213. Just like that time, I took this medicine, and...!!"
"That time?"
Those words made Tomoe suddenly catch her breath.
"...No way, you mean the assault in Ibaraki?!"
Reina's face turned completely pale... and she nodded.
When Tomoe heard that, it felt like the disparate puzzle pieces inside her head were finally snapping together.
Natsumi Kimiyoshi's sudden transformation.
Reina Ryuugu's assault incident in Ibaraki.
Even though the incidents seemed completely unrelated aside from the fact that the two of them were from Hinamizawa, there was a solid verifiable connection between them.
And the link binding them together... was this medication that Reina just referred to as "PC"...?!
"...Ryuugu-san!"
"Huh?@k@vS14/02/VT4Bb_rena1215. Y-yes?!"
"Do you mind if we take a small detour after this?@k@vS14/28/VT4Bb_tomoe2148. There's something I need your help with... Please!"
